name: Fragrant Eggplant
recipeIngredient: 6 slices ginger, about the size and thickness of a quarter
4 scallions, chopped, separate white and green parts
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off the stem end of the eggplant. Dice the eggplant into small cubes.
2. Sprinkle the diced eggplant with salt and place in a colander to drain. Let sit for 15 minutes. Squeeze as much liquid out as possible.
3. In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, sugar, vinegar, and water.
4. Heat 1 tablespoon dry sherry in a large skillet or wok. Add red peppers and stir. Add ginger and the white part of scallion. Stir fry briefly until the ginger becomes fragrant. Add the squeezed eggplant and sauté for approximately 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the eggplant is thoroughly cooked. If it sticks, add a little bit of water or sherry.
5. Add the soy sauce mixture and cook over high heat until most of the liquid has evaporated and the eggplant is thoroughly coated with the reduced sauce, about 5 minutes.
6. Combine 2 tablespoons sherry with cornstarch in another small bowl.
7. Add chopped green part of scallion and the sherry mixed with cornstarch to the skillet. Stir and cook until thick. Serve hot over plain rice.
